search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae acute low back pain Issue 2 history red flags cauda equina imaging referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Undifferentiated ED back pain includes traumatic, vascular, infectious, malignant, renal and neurological causes.
No investigation, medicine, dose, referral or escalation was auto-generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with acute low back pain; pediatric, pregnancy, trauma, postoperative and non-lumbar presentations require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: DHA virtual-care history and red-flag domains partially transfer to emergency documentation; in-person examination and acute pathways remain local.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA evidence, with UAE emergency imaging, neurosurgical, orthopaedic, urological and referral pathways required.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact source was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA acute low-back-pain history section supports onset, duration, severity, functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and risk context without asserting a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports acute low-back-pain terminology and pain site, onset, duration, severity, character, radiation, aggravating factors, injury mechanism, prior treatment, function, and patient concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports recording time-critical symptoms only when the clinician assessed them.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists weight loss, fever, night sweats, malignancy, osteoporosis, weakness, gait instability, sensory change, bowel or bladder dysfunction, trauma, infection, injection drugs, corticosteroids, and recent spinal procedures.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact cauda-equina and imaging section supports documenting existing imaging or test status only when already performed or reviewed, without recommending imaging.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes cauda-equina symptom combinations and limits imaging in short-duration low back pain without associated concern features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports recording escalation, specialist discussion or arranged follow-up only when clinician-decided.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines urgent and specialist review for severe or night pain, infection, trauma, neurological deficit, urinary or faecal dysfunction, systemic disease, persistent symptoms, and selected imaging needs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Trauma, location/radiation, neurological and bladder/bowel detail, vascular or abdominal causes, examination, diagnosis, tests, treatment, reassessment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
